effect-smol.purpose: Read-only Effect v4 source pinned to the tag matching the effect catalog — the authority on what v4 actually exports, plus the v3→v4 migration notes and the @effect/vitest reference implementation. Vendored from the main Effect-TS/effect monorepo since 2026-07-19; v4 development moved back there when effect-smol was archived (same layout, directory name kept).

orientation.layout: Sparse checkout: packages/effect (core source + package.json exports map), packages/vitest (@effect/vitest reference implementation), migration/ (per-domain v3→v4 notes), ai-docs/, LLMS.md, MIGRATION.md.
orientation.startHere: packages/effect/src settles what v4 actually exports (evidence-ladder rung 2); migration/*.md settles v3→v4 renames (rung 1). Before trusting an answer, check the pin matches the installed effect version — node_modules wins on disagreement.

commonmark-js.purpose: Port base for @effected/markdown: the CommonMark reference parser (two-phase blocks/inlines algorithm, BSD-2) pinned at spec 0.31.2

orientation.layout: Sparse checkout: lib/ (the parser: blocks.js block pass, inlines.js inline pass, node.js AST, render/ excluded from the port), test/ (spec.txt copy, regression.txt, smart_punct.txt).
orientation.startHere: lib/blocks.js then lib/inlines.js — the two-phase algorithm the @effected/markdown engine ports. Port deltas are decided in the design doc (.claude/design/effected/packages/markdown.md): mdast node names, byte offsets, keep definitions/references unresolved. lib/render is out of scope (no product HTML).

commonmark-spec.purpose: CommonMark 0.31.2 spec text and conformance tooling (spec.txt, spec_tests.py, normalize.py) — source of the 652-example corpus for @effected/markdown

orientation.layout: Sparse checkout: spec.txt (the CommonMark 0.31.2 spec; 652 embedded examples), test/ (spec_tests.py extracts examples, normalize.py is the HTML-normalization reference for the test-only writer).
orientation.startHere: spec.txt's appendix 'A parsing strategy' is the algorithm contract. Extract the conformance corpus with spec_tests.py --dump-tests (or vendor the published spec.json); normalize.py defines the HTML equivalence the __test__/ harness reimplements. Test data is CC-BY-SA 4.0 — vendor test-only with attribution.

notes.note: Running test/spec_tests.py drops test/__pycache__/ into the checkout, which shows as dirty in repos status forever (the .repos tripwires block git clean inside the submodule, and sync does not fix dirt). Always invoke it as PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 test/spec_tests.py ... — avoiding the write is the only clean path. The existing __pycache__ from the 2026-07-18 corpus generation is harmless untracked bytecode.

cmark-gfm.purpose: GFM spec text and corpora (test/spec.txt extension sections, extensions.txt incl. footnotes, pathological tests) plus C reference implementations of the GFM constructs

orientation.layout: Sparse checkout: test/ (spec.txt with the GFM extension sections, extensions.txt incl. the only official footnote corpus, pathological_tests.py, fuzzing assets), extensions/ (C implementations: table, strikethrough, autolink, tasklist, tagfilter), src/ (cmark core incl. footnotes).
orientation.startHere: test/spec.txt extension sections (Tables/Task list items/Strikethrough/Autolinks/Disallowed Raw HTML, 24 examples) + test/extensions.txt (30 more, incl. footnotes) are the P2 corpora; test/pathological_tests.py is the linear-time guarantee suite every dialect must pass. extensions/*.c are semantic references only — the port base is commonmark.js, not this C.

mdast.purpose: The mdast spec prose (readme.md IS the spec) — the node-type contract @effected/markdown's Schema classes mirror, incl. the GFM extension node set

orientation.layout: Full checkout; readme.md is the entire spec (unist-based node types, mixins, enumerations).
orientation.startHere: readme.md — the 19 CommonMark node types plus the GFM section (delete, table/tableRow/tableCell, listItem.checked, footnoteDefinition/footnoteReference) are the names and field shapes MarkdownNode.ts must mirror exactly; the Mdast projection is spec-valid only if these match.

mdast-util-from-markdown.purpose: Reference for event-to-mdast tree assembly and the 27 position-complete .md/.json AST fixtures used as @effected/markdown's interop corpus

orientation.layout: Sparse checkout: dev/lib/index.js (the readable event-to-mdast assembler), test/index.js (the CommonMark-conformance-without-product-HTML harness precedent), test/fixtures/ (27 .md/.json pairs with full unist positions).
orientation.startHere: test/fixtures/*.json are the position-complete mdast trees the Mdast projection is asserted against (P5 corpus). test/index.js shows the normalized-HTML conformance technique for a library that ships no HTML renderer — the pattern __test__/e2e reimplements.
